step_by_step_reasoning: Based on the whiteboard image and transcript, we identify the following components: 1) Meta Manager running on EKS which acts as the core controller. 2) Elasticsearch and Neo4j used as the MetaStore. 3) Multiple Data Sources feeding metadata to EKS and streaming data to Kafka. 4) Kafka (MSK) managing 600 topics of streaming ingestion. 5) EMR processing data alongside S3 Data Lake (60TB). 6) RDS and Vertica (running on EC2) serving as analytical and transactional databases. 7) Downstream Applications consuming/producing data. 8) Data Analysts / Data Scientists (UserCompanyAnalyst) utilizing the data. Edges are created strictly matching the physical arrows on the board.

graph.name: Majid Al Futtaim Multi-Cloud Analytics & Customer Data Platform
graph.notes: Builds a unified data platform ('the grid') to aggregate data across multiple countries and business verticals (retail, cinema, real estate). Automates infrastructure spin-up/down using DevOps practices.

nodes.name: Meta Manager
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Brains of the grid, abstracts complexity of data movement. Runs as microservices on EKS.

nodes.name: MetaStore
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Labeled Elasticsearch/Neo4j. Stores schema, API, and table details.

nodes.name: Multiple Data Sources
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Legacy databases and APIs across different countries and business domains.

nodes.name: Kafka
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Managed Kafka streaming platform with 600 topics, 250 GB/day, and 7 days retention.

nodes.name: S3 Data Lake
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Labeled 60TB. Central repository for multi-source data.

nodes.name: EMR
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Spark/Hadoop processing engine to aggregate and refine data lake tables.

nodes.name: RDS
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Target transactional OLTP database for consumed analytics.

nodes.name: Vertica
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Labeled Vertica. Columnar database running on EC2 for high-performance analytics.

nodes.name: Applications
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Consumer-facing applications, loyalty systems, and notification engines.

nodes.name: DA/DS
nodes.notes: Data Analysts and Data Scientists consuming query platforms.

edges.notes: EKS Meta Manager discovers metadata from source tables
Streams raw source events into Kafka
Persists streaming records to S3 Data Lake
Loads data into EMR for heavy processing
Saves structured and optimized files back to S3
Data scientists build predictive models on Vertica
Feeds calculated profiles and segments to applications
Application metadata and status loop back
